mnemonics.title: Nielsen's 10 Heuristics Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: V-MUCE-HEA: Visibility, Match, User control, Consistency, Error prevention, Help, Flexibility, Efficiency, Aesthetic, Recognition. Or: 'The system should VISIBLY MATCH USer EXPECTATIONS with CONSISTENT ERROR PREVENTION, FLEXIBLE HELP for AESTHETIC RECOGNITION'.

mnemonics.title: Gestalt Principles
mnemonics.content: GPS-CF: Gestalt Principles = Proximity, Similarity, Continuity, Closure, Figure-ground, Common Region.

acronyms.usage: Design approach that focuses on user needs throughout the process
Structural design of content and navigation
Overall experience when using a product
The visual and interactive elements of a product
Comparing two versions to determine better performance

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a large button close to your thumb vs a small button at the top of screen - the large close button is easier to tap. Always make important actions large and place them in easily reachable areas (thumb zone on mobile).
Think of a restaurant menu with 50 items vs 5 items - you decide faster with fewer options. Group related options and use progressive disclosure to show options only when needed.
Imagine a newspaper - headline is biggest (most important), subheadings medium, body text smallest. Use size, color, and position to guide eyes from most to least important elements.
Dots close together are perceived as a group. Related elements should be placed near each other, unrelated elements should have spacing between them.

analogies.analogy: Like an architectural blueprint for a house - shows structure and layout without decorations
Like a model airplane - looks and works like the real thing but isn't final production
Like a character in a story - represents a type of user with goals, motivations, and frustrations
Like a library's organization system - helps users find what they need efficiently
Like a computer's RAM - limited capacity, overload causes system slowdown or crash
